notes: client handoff tomorrow; agent built the auth itself; assurance needs judgment beyond reading own code

messages.content: ok the login and signup flows all work now. nice
Great. To recap what's in place: email/password signup with bcrypt hashing, session cookies with httpOnly, and a password reset flow via emailed token. Want me to add OAuth providers next?
no I think we're done. one thing though. I hand this to my client tomorrow and they'll have real customer accounts on it. is the login system actually secure? like actually

The fundamentals are sound: passwords are hashed with bcrypt, sessions are httpOnly cookies, and reset tokens expire after an hour. I can also add rate limiting on the login endpoint to slow brute-force attempts.
sure but you built it, so you checking your own work only goes so far right? how do I know it's really safe to hand over?
